skill.name: performance-fundamentals
skill.description: Auto-invoke when reviewing loops, data fetching, rendering, database queries, or resource-intensive operations. Identifies N+1 queries, unnecessary re-renders, memory leaks, and scalability issues.
skill.summary: Review code for database, rendering, API, memory, and scalability performance issues.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown examples as shell execution, a prose mention of localStorage, and a weak cryptography issue. Review found no executable scripts, network access, credential access, prompt injection, or cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged locations are Markdown code fences or inline complexity examples used to teach performance review. They do not define executable scripts or instruct the agent to run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain Markdown documentation and examples, not runnable skill code. No command execution primitive or user input path is present.

low_findings.title: Browser Storage Mention Is Performance Guidance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The localStorage reference is a red flag to ask about large client-side JSON. It does not access browser storage or collect stored data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line appears in a review checklist table and is phrased as a question. There is no JavaScript storage API call or exfiltration path.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Has No Code Evidence
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: No evidence found for weak cryptographic algorithms in the skill. The referenced line is frontmatter describing performance review triggers.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found no MD5, SHA1, hashing, encryption, or credential handling. Confidence is high because the file is a short Markdown-only skill.

content.value_statement: Teams often miss slow queries, render churn, and memory leaks during routine review. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused performance checks.
actual_capabilities: Reviews database access for N+1 queries, missing indexes, pagination, and oversized selections.
Checks React and Vue rendering patterns for memoization, callback stability, and repeated computation.
Evaluates API response size, compression, cache headers, and async processing choices.
Identifies cleanup gaps for timers, subscriptions, event listeners, and database connections.
Provides Socratic questions that help junior developers reason about scale and resource use.
limitations: Does not run benchmarks, profilers, or load tests by itself.
Does not inspect files unless the host AI tool reads them.
Provides guidance, not guaranteed performance measurements.
Examples focus on common web and database applications.

use_cases.title: Review Database Changes
use_cases.description: Find N+1 queries, missing pagination, broad selects, and indexing gaps before a pull request is approved.

use_cases.title: Audit Render Performance
use_cases.description: Check component updates, expensive render work, memoization choices, and long-list behavior in frontend code.

use_cases.title: Coach Junior Reviewers
use_cases.description: Use structured questions to teach developers how to reason about scale, payload size, and resource cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Performance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the performance-fundamentals skill to review this change for obvious performance risks. Focus on queries, loops, rendering, and cleanup.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small pull request

prompt_templates.title: Frontend Render Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these components for unnecessary re-renders, expensive render work, stale callbacks, and missing cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: API Scalability Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this API path for response size, pagination, caching headers, database access, and slow synchronous work.

prompt_templates.title: Full Performance Gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a release-blocking performance review. Rank risks by user impact, evidence, likely scale, and remediation effort.

output_examples.input: Review a user list endpoint before launch.
output: High risk: The endpoint may return too many records without pagination.
Medium risk: The response includes fields that the list page does not display.
Next step: Add pagination, select only displayed fields, and confirm database indexes.

output_examples.input: Review a dashboard component that refreshes data.
output: Potential issue: The refresh timer needs explicit cleanup when the component unmounts.
Potential issue: Sorting large arrays during render can block interaction.
Next step: Move expensive work behind memoization and verify refresh cleanup.

best_practices: Review expected data volume before judging whether an approach is acceptable.
Ask for evidence from traces, logs, metrics, or profiling when impact is unclear.
Rank findings by user impact and remediation effort.
anti_patterns: Treating every optimization suggestion as release blocking without evidence.
Ignoring database query count because local test data is small.
Adding caching before fixing avoidable over-fetching or missing pagination.

faq.question: Does this skill execute performance tests?
faq.answer: No. It guides review and analysis, but it does not run benchmarks or profilers.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this only for frontend code?
faq.answer: No. It covers frontend rendering, backend APIs, database access, and resource cleanup.

faq.question: Can it prove a change is fast enough?
faq.answer: No. It can identify risks, but production metrics or controlled tests should confirm performance.

faq.question: Does it include mentoring guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Socratic questions for teaching developers how to reason about performance.

faq.question: What review issues does it emphasize?
faq.answer: It emphasizes N+1 queries, missing pagination, unnecessary renders, large payloads, and missing cleanup.
